product_overview: ColdGuard is an autonomous cold chain breach monitoring and recovery system for temperature-sensitive shipments. It combines FastAPI, Firebase, and multi-agent LLM orchestration to detect breaches, analyze risk, and execute containment actions in under 30 seconds.
core_goals: Detect temperature breaches in real time across active truck sensors and shipment records.
Analyze unstructured and structured breach inputs to identify affected trucks and cargo.
Run a sequential AI agent pipeline to assess risk, decide recovery actions, and execute containment.
Update Firebase-backed operational state and surface live incident progress to client applications.
Provide resilient fallback behavior so breach resolution continues even when the LLM service is unavailable.

features.name: System Health and Live Fleet Monitoring
features.description: Provides operational health visibility plus real-time access to truck sensor readings and shipment status records.
user_flows: GET / with no authentication -> Receive 200 health status response -> GET /sensors to fetch all truck sensor readings -> Receive 200 sensor list with current temperatures and statuses -> GET /shipments to fetch all shipments -> Receive 200 shipment list with current shipment states
GET /sensors -> Receive 200 with sensor data for trucks currently in normal operation -> GET /shipments -> Receive 200 with active shipment records and statuses

GET / with server unavailable -> Receive error response or no response indicating backend downtime -> Retry GET /sensors or GET /shipments after recovery -> Receive 200 responses with current Firebase-backed data

features.name: Structured Breach Trigger and Multi-Agent Resolution Pipeline
features.description: Simulates a temperature breach for a specific truck and runs the full four-agent containment pipeline to produce an execution trace.
user_flows: POST /trigger-breach with valid truck_id and temperature above threshold -> Receive 200 AgentPipelineTrace containing sensor assessment, analysis, decision, and execution results -> Shipment status and incident data are written to Firebase -> Client can refresh GET /shipments to see updated quarantine status
POST /trigger-breach with a truck_id that does not exist -> Receive 404 Truck not found -> No breach pipeline is executed -> GET /shipments remains unchanged

POST /trigger-breach with valid truck_id but database update failure during execution -> Receive 500 Database update failed -> Pipeline trace indicates partial failure -> GET /shipments may still show the prior shipment state depending on write completion

features.name: Unstructured Breach Analysis and Extraction
features.description: Parses free-text driver reports or field notes, extracts truck and temperature data, and then triggers the breach response workflow.
user_flows: POST /analyze-unstructured with content containing a truck identifier and temperature statement -> Receive 200 UnstructuredAnalysisResult -> Extracted truck_id and temperature are used to trigger the same breach response pipeline -> Updated incident and shipment state is available through GET /shipments

POST /analyze-unstructured with malformed content that omits truck_id or temperature -> Receive 200 UnstructuredAnalysisResult with missing or failed extraction details -> Breach response is not triggered or is triggered with fallback handling according to extraction result

POST /analyze-unstructured with ambiguous or noisy text -> Receive 200 result with parsed values if extraction succeeds -> If extraction fails, receive result indicating inability to confidently identify breach inputs

features.name: Live Agent Trace Streaming and Incident Visibility
features.description: Streams the step-by-step execution of the AI agents so operators and client apps can follow breach resolution in real time.
user_flows: GET /agent-trace/stream/{truck_id}/{temperature} -> Receive streamed pipeline_started event -> Receive streamed agent_started and agent_completed events for each agent -> Receive pipeline_completed event with full incident summary

GET /agent-trace/stream/{truck_id}/{temperature} while the pipeline is interrupted -> Receive partial stream events up to the failure point -> Stream ends before pipeline_completed -> Client can fall back to the stored trace data after refreshing the incident state
GET /agent-trace/stream/{truck_id}/{temperature} for an unknown truck or invalid input -> Receive stream error or no valid pipeline events -> No agent execution trace is produced

features.name: Demo Reset and Baseline Recovery
features.description: Restores the system to a clean demonstration state by clearing incidents and resetting trucks and shipments to their original baseline configuration.
user_flows: GET /reset-demo -> Receive success response after clearing the /incidents database reference -> All trucks and shipments are reset to baseline values -> GET /sensors and GET /shipments return clean demo-state data

GET /reset-demo during active monitoring -> Receive reset confirmation -> Previous breach incidents are removed from the operational view -> Subsequent GET /shipments no longer shows quarantined demo records

GET /reset-demo when the database reset fails -> Receive error response from backend or storage layer -> Incidents and fleet state may remain partially unchanged -> Retry after database service recovery

features.name: Cold Chain Breach Monitoring API
features.description: Health checking and real-time cold chain temperature monitoring endpoints.

endpoints.description: Returns all truck sensor readings from Firebase
Returns all shipments from Firebase with current status

features.name: AI Multi-Agent Breach Resolution API
features.description: Trigger breach simulation and execute LLM orchestrator-agent containment pipeline.

endpoints.description: Simulate a sensor reading a temperature breach and execute 4-agent containment pipeline
Analyzes unstructured text report to extract truck_id and temperature and trigger breach response
